info.title: Flashduty Open API
info.description: Public HTTP API for the Flashduty incident management platform — incidents, notification templates, channels, schedules, monitors, RUM, and platform administration. Every operation is authenticated with an `app_key` query parameter issued from the Flashduty console under Account → APP Keys. Responses follow a uniform envelope: `{ request_id, data }` on success, `{ request_id, error }` on failure.

tags.name: Monitors/Alert rules
tags.description: Create, manage, and export monitor alert rules. Query rule counters and audit history.

tags.name: Monitors/Data sources
tags.description: Manage monitoring data sources used by alert rules to query metrics.

tags.name: Monitors/Rule sets
tags.description: Manage shared rule sets (rulesets) in the Monitors rule repository. Rulesets can be shared publicly or within an account.

post.description: Update the note, sharing flag, and payload of an existing ruleset.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Rule Repository Manage** (`monit`) |

## Usage

- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Return the stored time series of the total rule count across the account — one sample per `clock` timestamp.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Alerting Rules Read** (`monit`) |

## Usage

- Each item is a historical snapshot: `num` is the total rule count at the given `clock` (Unix epoch seconds).

post.description: Create a new monitoring data source. The `payload` must include the type-specific configuration block.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Datasources Manage** (`monit`) |

## Usage

- `type_ident` must be one of: `prometheus`, `loki`, `mysql`, `oracle`, `postgres`, `clickhouse`, `elasticsearch`, `sls`, `victorialogs`.
- `edge_cluster_name` specifies which Monitors edge cluster evaluates rules using this datasource.
- For `elasticsearch`, set `payload.elasticsearch.deployment` to `cloud` or `self-managed`.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Return the audit record (including the `content` field, a JSON string of the rule snapshot at that point in time).

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Alerting Rules Read** (`monit`) |

## Usage

- Pass the audit record `id` (not the rule `id`) from `POST /monit/rule/audits`.
- `content` is a JSON string — parse it to get the full rule snapshot.

| Permissions | **Alerting Rules Manage** (`monit`) |

## Usage

- Set `folder_id` to `0` to get summary across all folders.
- If the folder contains too many rules, computation is skipped for self-protection.

post.description: Import one or more alert rules from a JSON array. Returns the result for each rule, indicating success or failure.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **20 requests/minute**; **2 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Alerting Rules Manage** (`monit`) |

## Usage

- The request body is a JSON array of rule export objects (compatible with the output of `POST /monit/rule/export`).
- Each object must include `folder_id`, `ds_type`, and either `ds_list` or `ds_ids`.
- Some rules may fail (e.g. duplicate name). Check each result for individual status.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Create a new alert rule. Returns the created rule with its assigned ID.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Alerting Rules Manage** (`monit`) |

## Usage

- `name`, `ds_type`, `cron_pattern`, and `rule_configs.queries` are required.
- Either `ds_list` (supports wildcards) or `ds_ids` must be non-empty.
- `cron_pattern` uses standard 5-field cron syntax.
- `channel_ids` can be empty; alerts will then route through the global integration.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Return all rulesets for a given datasource type that are accessible to the current user.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Rule Repository Read** (`monit`) |

## Usage

- A ruleset is visible if its `open_flag` is `2` (public), if the current user is the creator (`open_flag=0`), or if it belongs to the same account (`open_flag=1`).
- The list does not include the `payload` field. Call `/monit/store/ruleset/info` to get the full ruleset content.

post.description: Update specific fields across multiple alert rules at once. Only the fields listed in `fields` are applied.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Alerting Rules Manage** (`monit`) |

## Usage

- Include the field names you want to update in the `fields` array, e.g. `["enabled", "channel_ids"]`.
- Only the specified fields are updated; others are left unchanged.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Return an object mapping channel name to the number of rules routing alerts to that channel. If a channel name cannot be resolved, the channel ID (as a string) is used as the key.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Alerting Rules Read** (`monit`) |

AppKeyAuth.name: app_key
AppKeyAuth.description: App key issued from the Flashduty console under Account → APP Keys. Required on every public API call. Keep it secret — it grants the same access as the owning account.

BadRequest.description: Invalid request — usually a missing or malformed parameter.

error.message: The specified parameter is not valid.

Forbidden.description: The app_key is valid but lacks permission for this operation.

NotFound.description: The referenced resource does not exist or has been deleted. Note: Flashduty historically returns HTTP 400 with code `ResourceNotFound` for missing domain entities; a true 404 is reserved for unknown routes.

error.message: The resource you request is not found

TooManyRequests.description: Rate limit hit. Either the global API limit, a per-account limit, or a per-integration limit.

ServerError.description: Unexpected server-side error. Include the request_id when reporting.

error.message: We encountered an internal error, and it has been reported. Please try again later.

cron_pattern.description: 5-field cron schedule. Must not start with `CRON_TZ=` or `TZ=`; use the `timezone` field instead.

timezone.description: Timezone in which the rule executes. Determines how the cron schedule and effective time windows are interpreted. Only IANA timezone names are accepted (e.g. `Asia/Shanghai`, `UTC`, `Europe/London`); shortcuts and offsets such as `Local`, `UTC+8`, or `CST` are rejected. Treated as `Asia/Shanghai` if empty.

enabled_times.description: Time windows when the rule is active.

channel_ids.description: Channel IDs to send alerts to.

AlertRuleAudit.description: An audit record capturing a rule snapshot at a point in time.

alert_rule_id.description: ID of the alert rule this record belongs to.

content.description: JSON string of the full rule snapshot at audit time. Populated on `/monit/rule/audit/detail`, omitted on list responses.

AlertRuleBasic.description: Basic alert rule information for list views.

cron_pattern.description: 5-field cron schedule, e.g. `* * * * *`. Must not start with `CRON_TZ=` or `TZ=`; use the `timezone` field instead.

timezone.description: Timezone in which the rule executes. Determines how the cron schedule and effective time windows are interpreted. Only IANA timezone names are accepted (e.g. `Asia/Shanghai`, `UTC`, `Europe/London`); shortcuts and offsets such as `Local`, `UTC+8`, or `CST` are rejected. Treated as `Asia/Shanghai` if empty.

triggered.description: True if the rule currently has active alerts.

AlertRuleCounter.description: One historical snapshot of the account's alert rule total.

num.description: Rule count at the sample time.

AlertRuleExport.description: Portable alert rule representation for import/export. Omits identifying fields like `id`, `account_id`, and audit metadata.

timezone.description: Timezone in which the rule executes. IANA timezone name; defaults to `Asia/Shanghai`.

AlertRuleExportListResponse.description: List of exported rule configurations, compatible with `POST /monit/rule/import`.

AlertRuleInfoResponse.description: Full alert rule returned by the info endpoint. All server-assigned fields are guaranteed present.

AlertRuleStatus.description: Rule trigger status for a folder node.

rule_total.description: Total rules in the folder family.

DSClickHouseConfig.description: ClickHouse datasource configuration. TLS fields are inherited from TLSClientConfig.

max_execution_seconds.description: Max query execution time in seconds.

deployment.description: Deployment type. `cloud` uses Elastic Cloud; `self-managed` uses a self-hosted cluster.

cloud_id.description: Elastic Cloud deployment ID. Only for `cloud` deployment.

api_key.description: Elastic Cloud API key. Only for `cloud` deployment.

service_token.description: Service token; overrides username/password if set.

DSLokiConfig.description: Loki datasource configuration. TLS fields are inherited from TLSClientConfig.

DSMySQLConfig.description: MySQL datasource configuration. TLS fields are inherited from TLSClientConfig.

options.description: Extra connection options as key-value pairs.

DSPayload.description: Type-specific datasource configuration. Include only the block matching `type_ident`.

DSPrometheusConfig.description: Prometheus datasource configuration. TLS fields are inherited from TLSClientConfig.

headers.description: Custom HTTP headers in `Key: Value` format.

DSType.description: A datasource type definition usable by alert rules.

ident.description: Identifier used as the `ds_type` of rules, e.g. `prometheus`.

account_id.description: Owning account ID. `0` for global types.

weight.description: Display order weight; higher appears first.

DSVictoriaLogsConfig.description: VictoriaLogs datasource configuration. TLS fields are inherited from TLSClientConfig.

address.description: Connection address. For Prometheus/Loki/VictoriaLogs: HTTP URL. For MySQL/Oracle/Postgres/ClickHouse: `host:port`. For SLS: endpoint without http/https prefix.

edge_cluster_name.description: Monitors edge cluster name responsible for evaluating rules using this datasource.

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp, Unix epoch seconds.

type.description: Filter by datasource type identifier. Omit to return all types. Allowed values: `prometheus`, `loki`, `mysql`, `oracle`, `postgres`, `clickhouse`, `elasticsearch`, `sls`, `victorialogs`.

DataSourceListResponse.description: List of datasources. Sensitive credential fields are omitted.

DataSourceUpsertRequest.description: Request body for creating or updating a datasource. `id` is required only for update. `address` is required for all types except Elasticsearch with `deployment=cloud`.

id.description: Datasource ID. Required for update; omit for create.

address.description: Connection address. For Prometheus/Loki/VictoriaLogs: HTTP URL. For MySQL/Oracle/Postgres/ClickHouse: `host:port`. For SLS: endpoint without http/https prefix. Not required for Elasticsearch cloud deployment.

payload.description: Type-specific configuration block. Must include the key matching `type_ident`.

edge_cluster_name.description: Monitors edge cluster name responsible for evaluating rules using this datasource.

DutyError.description: Error payload inside the response envelope. Present only on non-2xx responses.

message.description: Human-readable error message, localized by the caller's Accept-Language. May contain field names, IDs, or other context from the failing request.
message.example: The specified parameter template_id is not valid.

EmptyObject.description: An empty object. Returned as the `data` payload by operations whose success signal is simply the absence of an error.

EmptyResponse.description: Empty response body. The server returns `data: null` on success.

EnabledTime.description: Time window in which the rule is active.

ErrorCode.description:
Flashduty error code enum. Every failed API response sets `error.code` to one of these stable wire strings. HTTP status is informational — the authoritative signal is the enum value.

| Code | HTTP | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `OK` | 200 | Reserved — not returned on real errors. |
| `InvalidParameter` | 400 | A required parameter is missing or failed validation. |
| `BadRequest` | 400 | Generic 400 used when no more specific code fits. |
| `InvalidContentType` | 400 | The `Content-Type` header is not `application/json`. |
| `ResourceNotFound` | 400 | The referenced resource does not exist. Note: returned as HTTP 400, not 404 (historical choice). |
| `NoLicense` | 400 | The feature is license-gated and no active license was found. |
| `ReferenceExist` | 400 | Deletion blocked — other entities still reference this resource. |
| `Unauthorized` | 401 | `app_key` is missing, invalid, or expired. |
| `BalanceNotEnough` | 402 | Billing-gated operation with insufficient account balance. |
| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Authenticated but lacking the permission required for this operation. |
| `RouteNotFound` | 404 | The request URL path is not a known route. |
| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | The HTTP method is not allowed on this otherwise-known path. |
| `UndonedOrderExist` | 409 | An outstanding billing order blocks this new one. Wait and retry. |
| `RequestLocked` | 423 | Operation temporarily locked due to repeated failures. |
| `EntityTooLarge` | 413 | Request body exceeds the configured max size. |
| `RequestTooFrequently` | 429 | Rate limit hit — API-global, per-account, or per-integration. |
| `RequestVerifyRequired` | 428 | Second-factor verification required but not supplied. |
| `DangerousOperation` | 428 | High-risk operation requires MFA verification. |
| `InternalError` | 500 | Unhandled server-side error. Include `request_id` in the bug report. |
| `ServiceUnavailable` | 503 | A backend dependency is unavailable. Try again later. |

x-enumDescriptions.OK: Reserved — not returned on real errors.
x-enumDescriptions.InvalidParameter: A required parameter is missing or failed validation.
x-enumDescriptions.BadRequest: Generic 400 used when no more specific code fits.
x-enumDescriptions.ResourceNotFound: The referenced resource does not exist. Note: returned as HTTP 400, not 404 (historical choice).
x-enumDescriptions.NoLicense: The feature is license-gated and no active license was found.
x-enumDescriptions.ReferenceExist: Deletion blocked — other entities still reference this resource.
x-enumDescriptions.BalanceNotEnough: Billing-gated operation with insufficient account balance.
x-enumDescriptions.AccessDenied: Authenticated but lacking the permission required for this operation.
x-enumDescriptions.RouteNotFound: The request URL path is not a known route.
x-enumDescriptions.MethodNotAllowed: The HTTP method is not allowed on this otherwise-known path.
x-enumDescriptions.UndonedOrderExist: An outstanding billing order blocks this new one. Wait and retry.
x-enumDescriptions.RequestLocked: Operation temporarily locked due to repeated failures.
x-enumDescriptions.EntityTooLarge: Request body exceeds the configured max size.
x-enumDescriptions.RequestTooFrequently: Rate limit hit — API-global, per-account, or per-integration.
x-enumDescriptions.RequestVerifyRequired: Second-factor verification required but not supplied.
x-enumDescriptions.InternalError: Unhandled server-side error. Include `request_id` in the bug report.
x-enumDescriptions.ServiceUnavailable: A backend dependency is unavailable. Try again later.

ErrorResponse.description: Response envelope for errors. `error` is required; `data` is absent.

IDRequest.description: Request with a single numeric ID.

NameMessage.description: Per-item result for batch rule operations.

message.description: Empty on success, error message on failure.

RuleAuditListResponse.description: Audit records for a rule, ordered by creation time descending. The `content` field is omitted.

name.description: Query identifier (letter, e.g. `A`). The name `R` is reserved and must not be used.

relate_queries.description: Optional auxiliary queries whose results are attached to alert events as context. Each entry must have a unique `name` (not duplicating any query name) and a non-empty `expr`.

check_anydata.description: Any-data check configuration. Fires when the query returns any data rows.

recovery.description: Recovery condition for any-data check. If omitted or `mode` is empty, treated as `nodata`.

mode.description: `nodata` = recover when the query returns no data; `ql` = recover when the `condition` expression evaluates to true. When `mode` is `ql`, only a single query (`name=A`) is permitted.

RuleCounterChannelResponse.description: Map of channel name to rule count. Unresolved channel IDs appear as stringified IDs.

RuleCounterNodeResponse.description: Map of top-level folder name to rule count.

RuleCounterTotalResponse.description: Historical rule-count samples ordered by `clock` ascending.

RuleDsTypesResponse.description: Datasource types available to the account — global types plus account-scoped types.

timezone.description: Timezone in which the rule executes. IANA timezone name; defaults to `Asia/Shanghai`.

RuleImportRequest.description: Array of alert rule export objects to import.

RuleListRequest.description: Filter parameter for listing rules in a folder.

folder_id.description: Folder ID. 0 to list all accessible rules.

type_ident.description: Datasource type identifier this ruleset applies to.

note.description: Description or title of the ruleset.

open_flag.description: Sharing scope. `0` = private (creator only), `1` = account-shared, `2` = public.

payload.description: JSON string containing the alert rule definitions. Omitted in list responses.

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp, Unix epoch seconds.

type_ident.description: Datasource type identifier to filter by, e.g. `prometheus`.

StoreRulesetListResponse.description: Rulesets accessible to the current user. The `payload` field is omitted.

open_flag.description: New sharing scope. `0` = private, `1` = account-shared, `2` = public.

payload.description: New JSON string of alert rule definitions.

StoreRulesetUpsertRequest.description: Request body for creating a ruleset. All fields are validated by `Validate()` on the server.

type_ident.description: Datasource type identifier this ruleset applies to, e.g. `prometheus`.

note.description: Description or title of the ruleset.

open_flag.description: Sharing scope. `0` = private (creator only), `1` = account-shared, `2` = public. Defaults to `0` if omitted.

payload.description: JSON string containing the alert rule definitions.

SuccessEnvelope.description: Success response envelope. On every 2xx response, `request_id` identifies the call (also mirrored in the `Flashcat-Request-Id` header) and `data` holds the endpoint-specific payload. Failure responses use a different shape — see `ErrorResponse`.

request_id.description: Unique ID for this request. Mirrored in the Flashcat-Request-Id response header. Include it when reporting issues.

data.description: Endpoint-specific payload. See each operation's 200 response schema.
